rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly in the core theory framing, not just in a one-off background list. The citing paper draws on it to motivate the ex ante-ex post governance link (contract specificity/completeness vs. monitoring and compliance-opportunism outcomes) and to position prior work as generally viewing monitoring as efficiency enhancing. The new study then introduces boundary conditions in multicomponent IIOT settings, showing monitoring can raise disputes, which qualifies that prior logic.
relation_description: The citing paper builds from the governance logic represented by Kashyap, Antia, and Frazier (2012) on how contract design and ex post controls relate to channel behavior. It then narrows and qualifies that logic by showing that in industrial multicomponent systems, increased monitoring can generate a dispute penalty rather than only coordination benefits.
